name: BigQuery Advanced Features for Data Engineers
description: Advanced BigQuery capabilities including partitioning, clustering, authorized views, row-level security, and optimizing query performance and cost.
key_facts: Partitioning (by ingestion time, column date, or integer range) reduces bytes scanned and query cost significantly
Clustering organizes data within partitions by specified columns, improving filter and aggregation performance
Authorized views allow sharing query results without exposing underlying tables; row-level security uses row access policies
INFORMATION_SCHEMA views provide metadata about tables, partitions, jobs, and slot reservations for monitoring
Materialized views precompute and cache query results; BigQuery automatically refreshes them when base tables change
common_misconceptions: Clustering and partitioning have the same effect — partitioning reduces data scanned; clustering improves ordering within partitions for filter queries
More BigQuery slots always means faster queries — slots are units of compute; query performance also depends on data organization and SQL efficiency

name: Dataflow and Apache Beam for Stream and Batch Processing
description: Building and running Apache Beam pipelines on Dataflow for unified batch and streaming data processing with windowing, triggers, and side inputs.
key_facts: Apache Beam provides a unified programming model for batch (bounded) and streaming (unbounded) data pipelines
Windowing divides streaming data into finite chunks: Fixed (tumbling), Sliding (hopping), Session, and Global windows
Dataflow Shuffle service and Dataflow Prime optimize resource usage and reduce worker costs
Dataflow Flex Templates package pipeline code and dependencies in a container for portable, parameterized job submission
Watermarks track event-time progress in streaming pipelines; late data handling uses allowed lateness and side outputs
common_misconceptions: Dataflow only supports Java — Dataflow supports Python, Java, and Go Apache Beam SDKs
Processing time and event time are the same — event time is when the event occurred; processing time is when it arrives at the pipeline

name: Pub/Sub for Streaming Data Ingestion
description: Using Pub/Sub as a scalable, durable message ingestion layer for streaming data pipelines, including ordering, filtering, and schema enforcement.
key_facts: Pub/Sub provides global, durable, at-least-once message delivery with configurable retention (up to 7 days)
Schema registry in Pub/Sub enforces Avro or Protocol Buffer schemas on published messages
BigQuery subscriptions write Pub/Sub messages directly to BigQuery tables without Dataflow for simple pipelines
Message filtering on subscriptions reduces costs by delivering only matching messages to subscribers
Pub/Sub Lite is a zonal, lower-cost alternative for high-volume workloads where regional durability is not required
common_misconceptions: Pub/Sub automatically deduplicates messages — at-least-once delivery means duplicates can occur; consumers must handle idempotently
Pub/Sub subscriptions store messages indefinitely — message retention is configurable (10 minutes to 7 days) before messages expire

name: Dataproc for Managed Hadoop and Spark
description: Running Apache Spark, Hadoop, Flink, and Hive workloads on managed Dataproc clusters with autoscaling, serverless Spark, and Cloud Storage integration.
key_facts: Dataproc clusters create in 90 seconds; use ephemeral clusters for each job and store data in Cloud Storage (not HDFS)
Dataproc Serverless for Spark runs Spark batches without managing clusters; Google manages resources automatically
Dataproc Metastore provides a fully managed Hive Metastore for Spark, Hive, and other data processing frameworks
Preemptible VMs as secondary workers reduce Dataproc batch job costs by 60-80%
Cloud Storage connector enables Dataproc to read/write directly to Cloud Storage using the gs:// URI scheme
common_misconceptions: Dataproc is only for Hadoop workloads — it supports Spark (including Structured Streaming), Flink, Hive, Presto, and more
HDFS on Dataproc is recommended for production — use Cloud Storage for persistent storage; HDFS is only for temporary shuffle data

name: Bigtable for High-Throughput NoSQL Workloads
description: Designing Bigtable schemas for time-series, IoT, and analytics workloads with optimal row key design, column families, and performance tuning.
key_facts: Row key design is critical: reverse domain names (com.example vs example.com) prevent hotspots; avoid sequential keys
Column families group related columns; all columns in a family share the same garbage collection policy
Bigtable tables have one index (the row key); design row keys to support primary query patterns without table scans
Replication to multiple clusters in different regions provides low-latency global reads and disaster recovery
Key Visualizer tool identifies hotspots in row key access patterns to diagnose performance issues
common_misconceptions: Bigtable supports SQL — Bigtable uses an HBase-compatible API; it does not support SQL (use BigQuery for SQL analytics)
More column families improve performance — too many column families increase memory and storage overhead; use few families with many columns

name: Data Pipeline Architecture Design
description: Designing ELT/ETL data pipelines for batch and streaming workloads, selecting the right processing framework, and implementing data quality checks.
key_facts: ELT (Extract-Load-Transform) is preferred in modern cloud data stacks; load raw data first, transform in BigQuery
Cloud Composer (managed Apache Airflow) orchestrates complex multi-step DAG pipelines with dependencies and scheduling
Dataprep by Trifacta is a visual data preparation tool that generates Dataflow jobs for data cleaning and transformation
Data Catalog provides unified discovery, metadata management, and data lineage across GCP data assets
Data quality dimensions: completeness, consistency, accuracy, timeliness, validity — validate at ingestion and transformation stages
common_misconceptions: ETL is always better than ELT — ELT is preferred when the destination system (BigQuery) is powerful enough for transformation
Cloud Composer and Dataflow do the same thing — Composer orchestrates workflows; Dataflow executes data processing steps

name: Cloud Storage as a Data Lake
description: Using Cloud Storage as the foundation of a data lake with optimal file formats (Parquet, Avro, ORC), folder organization, and access control patterns.
key_facts: Parquet and ORC are columnar formats ideal for BigQuery external tables and Dataproc analytics; Avro is row-based and good for streaming
Organizing data by date (year/month/day prefix) enables partition discovery in BigQuery and Dataproc Hive
Cloud Storage Transfer Service and gsutil -m enable parallel multi-threaded transfers for large dataset migrations
Requester Pays enables cost attribution to the requester for large shared datasets
CMEK with Cloud KMS encrypts bucket data with customer-controlled keys for data sovereignty requirements
common_misconceptions: JSON is efficient for large analytical datasets — JSON is verbose; use Parquet or Avro for significantly better compression and query performance
Cloud Storage data is immediately queryable by BigQuery — external tables require Schema definition; load jobs offer better query performance

name: Cloud Spanner for Global ACID Transactions
description: Designing schemas and using Cloud Spanner for globally consistent, horizontally scalable relational data with interleaved tables and commit timestamps.
key_facts: Spanner uses TrueTime (GPS + atomic clocks) to assign globally consistent timestamps, enabling external consistency
Interleaved tables co-locate child rows with parent rows physically, avoiding expensive joins for parent-child queries
Primary key design: use UUIDs or bit-reversed sequence numbers to avoid write hotspots on sequential keys
Spanner Change Streams capture data changes in near-real-time for CDC (change data capture) pipelines via Dataflow
Spanner INFORMATION_SCHEMA provides metadata about tables, indexes, and columns for schema introspection
common_misconceptions: Spanner is just Cloud SQL scaled up — Spanner uses a fundamentally different architecture (Paxos consensus, TrueTime) enabling global consistency
Sequential primary keys are safe in Spanner — sequential keys cause write hotspots; use hashed or UUID keys

name: ML Integration for Data Engineers
description: Integrating ML capabilities into data pipelines using BigQuery ML, Vertex AI predictions, and feature engineering on GCP.
key_facts: BigQuery ML trains linear regression, logistic regression, k-means, and deep neural networks using SQL
Feature Store in Vertex AI stores and serves ML features for training and online prediction with low latency
Vertex AI Batch Prediction integrates with BigQuery for bulk scoring of large datasets
ML.PREDICT in BigQuery calls Vertex AI endpoints from BigQuery SQL for inline predictions
Feature engineering transforms raw data into model-ready features; common techniques include normalization, bucketing, and embedding
common_misconceptions: BigQuery ML requires Python or TensorFlow knowledge — it uses standard SQL for model training and prediction
Feature Store eliminates the need for feature engineering — Feature Store manages feature storage and serving, not the engineering itself

name: Data Security and Governance
description: Implementing data security controls including encryption, access control, data masking, and audit logging for sensitive data in GCP data systems.
key_facts: Cloud DLP (Data Loss Prevention) identifies, classifies, and redacts sensitive data (PII, PHI, PCI) in BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Datastore
BigQuery column-level security uses policy tags in Data Catalog to control access to sensitive columns
VPC Service Controls create data perimeters around BigQuery and Cloud Storage to prevent exfiltration
Cloud Audit Logs capture BigQuery job execution and data access events for compliance and forensics
Dataset and table ACLs in BigQuery control access; authorized views and authorized datasets enable controlled sharing
common_misconceptions: Encrypting a BigQuery dataset protects all its data — encryption protects data at rest; you still need column-level security and IAM for access control
DLP scanning is real-time — DLP jobs are typically batch scans; use DLP API for real-time content inspection in streaming pipelines

name: Cloud Composer Workflow Orchestration
description: Building and managing data pipeline DAGs in Cloud Composer (managed Apache Airflow) with GCP operators, XComs, and monitoring.
key_facts: Cloud Composer is fully managed Apache Airflow; DAGs are Python files stored in a Cloud Storage bucket
GCP operators (BigQueryInsertJobOperator, DataflowCreateJavaJobOperator, etc.) integrate pipelines with GCP services
XComs (cross-communications) pass small data between tasks; for large data use Cloud Storage as intermediate store
Cloud Composer 2 uses GKE Autopilot for autoscaling and reduced management overhead vs Composer 1
SLA miss callbacks and email alerts notify when DAG or task execution exceeds expected duration
common_misconceptions: Cloud Composer and Dataflow are interchangeable — Composer orchestrates which jobs to run and when; Dataflow processes the data within a job
Larger Composer environments always run DAGs faster — DAG parsing speed depends on Python code quality; over-provisioning increases cost

name: Real-Time Streaming Analytics Architecture
description: Designing end-to-end real-time analytics pipelines from event ingestion through Pub/Sub, processing with Dataflow, and serving via BigQuery.
key_facts: Reference streaming architecture: Source -> Pub/Sub -> Dataflow -> BigQuery (analytical) + Bigtable (operational serving)
BigQuery Storage Write API supports exactly-once and at-least-once streaming into BigQuery at high throughput
Dataflow streaming jobs use autoscaling to handle traffic spikes; monitor with Cloud Monitoring metrics
Change Data Capture (CDC) from Cloud SQL/Spanner using Datastream replicates changes to BigQuery in real time
Real-time dashboards with Looker Studio and BigQuery BI Engine enable sub-second query performance on streaming data
common_misconceptions: BigQuery supports real-time transactional queries — BigQuery is optimized for analytical queries; for real-time operational queries use Bigtable or Spanner
Datastream and Dataflow are alternatives — Datastream handles CDC replication; Dataflow handles general data transformation

name: Data Pipeline Monitoring and Troubleshooting
description: Monitoring data pipeline health, setting up SLO-based alerts, debugging failed jobs, and implementing data quality validation in production.
key_facts: Dataflow monitoring UI shows job graph, step metrics, throughput, and worker utilization for debugging
Cloud Monitoring custom metrics and dashboards track data pipeline SLIs (records processed, latency, error rate)
BigQuery job INFORMATION_SCHEMA tracks query performance, bytes billed, and slot utilization trends
Dead letter patterns in Dataflow route failed records to separate PubSub topic or BigQuery table for investigation
Cloud Composer task retry policies and failure callbacks handle transient failures in orchestration pipelines
common_misconceptions: A successful Dataflow job means data quality is good — a job can complete successfully while silently dropping or misprocessing records
Monitoring is only needed for streaming jobs — batch pipelines also need monitoring for duration SLOs and data completeness

name: Data Migration and Modernization
description: Migrating on-premises data warehouses, Hadoop clusters, and relational databases to GCP using appropriate migration tools and strategies.
key_facts: BigQuery Migration Service assesses, translates SQL, and migrates Teradata, Redshift, and Snowflake workloads to BigQuery
Dataproc migration from on-premises Hadoop: move data to Cloud Storage first, then run same Spark/Hive jobs on Dataproc
Database Migration Service (DMS) handles homogeneous (MySQL->Cloud SQL) and heterogeneous (Oracle->AlloyDB) migrations
Datastream provides continuous CDC replication from Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL to BigQuery or Cloud Storage
Transfer Appliance (hardware) ships petabytes offline when network bandwidth makes online transfer impractical (>100 TB)
common_misconceptions: Migrating to BigQuery requires rewriting all SQL — BigQuery Migration Service includes automated SQL translation for common dialects
Online database migrations always cause downtime — DMS uses logical replication to migrate with minimal downtime

name: Data Governance and Catalog
description: Implementing data governance with Data Catalog for metadata management, data discovery, business glossary, and lineage tracking across GCP data assets.
key_facts: Data Catalog automatically indexes metadata from BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Pub/Sub, and third-party sources
Business metadata (descriptions, tags, business terms) enriches technical metadata for data discoverability
Policy tags in Data Catalog enforce column-level access control in BigQuery for sensitive data classification
Data lineage in Dataplex tracks how data flows through pipelines for compliance and impact analysis
Dataplex provides unified data management with data quality rules, metadata, and automatic catalog integration
common_misconceptions: Data Catalog is a data store — it is a metadata catalog only; it does not store the actual data
Dataplex replaces Data Catalog — Dataplex incorporates Data Catalog as part of a broader data management platform
